---
title: "Citation"
---

## How to Cite

Prudencio, D. (2025). International child sponsorship impact on the intended choice of acquiring a higher education degree: the case of rural Mexico. *Education Economics*. https://doi.org/10.1080/09645292.2025.2498639

**BibTeX:**

```bibtex
@article{prudencio2025childsponsorship,
  author  = {Prudencio, Daniel},
  title   = {International child sponsorship impact on the intended choice
             of acquiring a higher education degree: the case of rural {Mexico}},
  journal = {Education Economics},
  year    = {2025},
  doi     = {10.1080/09645292.2025.2498639},
  note    = {Published online: 17 May 2025}
}
```

---

## Key Reference

The structural model follows:

> Aakvik, A., Heckman, J. J., & Vytlacil, E. J. (2005). Estimating treatment effects for discrete outcomes when responses to treatment vary: An application to Norwegian vocational rehabilitation programs. *Journal of Econometrics*, 125(1–2), 15–51.

The subjective expectations methodology follows:

> Attanasio, O. P., & Kaufmann, K. M. (2014). Education choices and returns to schooling: Mothers' and youths' subjective expectations and their role in education decisions. *Journal of Development Economics*, 109, 203–216.

---
